stats_fallback.christened: 2022-11-13 (Grand Cruise Terminal, Doha, Qatar — godmother Sheikha Hind bint Hamad Al Thani, Vice-Chairperson and CEO of the Qatar Foundation; notably broke MSC's long-standing Sophia Loren tradition)
stats_fallback.cabins_note: 2,626 cabins including 152 MSC Yacht Club suites. 1,127 separate crew cabins.
stats_fallback.power: LNG dual-fuel: five Wärtsilä 14V46DF engines (80 MW total); first contemporary cruise ship with a 150 kW Solid Oxide Fuel Cell (SOFC) demonstrator powered by LNG
stats_fallback.eedi: ~47% better than IMO EEDI requirement at delivery
stats_fallback.godmother: Sheikha Hind bint Hamad Al Thani (Vice-Chairperson and CEO, Qatar Foundation)
stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Installed from 2022 delivery. 152 Yacht Club suites forming the forward enclave with private MSC Yacht Club Restaurant, panoramic Top Sail Lounge, Grill & Bar, and private One Pool Deck. Butler service and 24-hour concierge.

related.class_notes: World Class. MSC World Europa is the lead and first ship. Sisters are MSC World America (2025, Miami-based) and MSC World Asia (scheduled November 2026). MSC World Atlantic is planned for February 2027. All ships are built by Chantiers de l'Atlantique at Saint-Nazaire and share the class-defining Y-shaped aft with floor-to-ceiling glass walls, LNG dual-fuel propulsion, the seven-deck outdoor World Promenade, the 11-deck Venom Drop @ The Spiral dry slide, and full MSC Yacht Club enclaves. MSC World Europa was the world's largest LNG dual-fuel cruise ship at her October 2022 delivery, and the first contemporary cruise ship with Solid Oxide Fuel Cell technology.

deployment.2023_onwards: Year-round 7-night Western Mediterranean round-trips since April 2023, calling at Genoa, Naples, Messina, Valletta (Malta), Barcelona, and Marseille.
deployment.current_ais_2026_04_11: Moored at Marseille, France, arrived 05:18 UTC on 11 April 2026. Previous port Barcelona (departed 10 April 16:52 UTC). Draught 9.3 m. (VesselFinder live data)
deployment.winter_2026_27: Tentative — sources conflict between continued Mediterranean/Gulf vs. a planned/retracted Caribbean redeployment. Awaiting MSC confirmation.

history.doha_arrival: 10 November 2022 — arrived Doha, Qatar to serve as floating hotel for 2022 FIFA World Cup
history.christened: 13 November 2022, Grand Cruise Terminal, Doha, Qatar (Sheikha Hind bint Hamad Al Thani). Ceremony hosted by Hamad Al Amari; tenor Matteo Bocelli performed solo in Doha for the first time; drone show, hull video mapping, fireworks. Partnership with Qatar Airways.
history.first_commercial_cruise: 20 December 2022 — 9-day round-trip from Qatar during FIFA World Cup accommodation period
history.2023_04_mediterranean: April 2023 — repositioned to year-round Western Mediterranean
history.2025_08_electrical_incident: August 2025 — reported electrical breakdown while sailing between Genoa and Naples (per CruiseMapper). No injuries reported in sources reviewed.

discrepancies_flagged.imo_contamination: Previous page had IMO 9837582 — which does not match MSC World Europa in any AIS database. Correct IMO is 9837420 per 3+ primary sources (VesselFinder, MarineTraffic, CruiseMapper, Chantiers de l'Atlantique). This is the NINTH IMO cross-contamination caught across the MSC fleet repair (after Bellissima, Euribia, Magnifica, Meraviglia, Musica/Orchestra swap, Seascape-vs-tug-LUBRANI, Seaside/Seaview sister-pair swap, and Sinfonia).
discrepancies_flagged.gt_hallucination: Previous page had GT 205,700 — likely from an older/rounded Cruise Industry News figure. Correct GT is 215,863 per five independent primary sources including Chantiers de l'Atlantique's own press release.
discrepancies_flagged.guests_hallucination: Previous page had '6,762 guests at double occupancy' and '5,264 guests' in different places — both wrong. Verified: 5,079 double occupancy / 6,762 maximum.
discrepancies_flagged.class_name_placeholder: Previous page had 'exceptional' as a placeholder class name in multiple places instead of 'World Class'. Fixed throughout.
discrepancies_flagged.cirque_hallucination_removed: Previous Review JSON-LD claimed 'Cirque du Soleil at Sea' on MSC World Europa. This is wrong — MSC World Europa was never part of the Cirque partnership, and the entire MSC × Cirque du Soleil at Sea partnership has since been phased out. Removed.
discrepancies_flagged.cliffhanger_hallucination_removed: Previous page claimed 'Cliffhanger over-water swing ride' — that is not on MSC World Europa. Removed.
discrepancies_flagged.led_dome_confusion: Previous page described 'the world's longest LED Dome at sea, spanning 104 meters'. The 104-metre figure is the length of the SEVEN-DECK OUTDOOR WORLD PROMENADE along the Y-shaped stern, not an LED dome. The LED-dome is the indoor WORLD GALLERIA ceiling. Clarified in corrected content.
discrepancies_flagged.europa_grand_canyon: Previous page referenced a '11-deck Europa Grand Canyon' atrium. The 11-deck feature on MSC World Europa is the VENOM DROP @ THE SPIRAL dry slide (Deck 20 to Deck 8), not an atrium. No 'Grand Canyon' atrium is documented in MSC's own materials. Removed.
discrepancies_flagged.six_waterslides: Previous page claimed 'six waterslides'. The current sources confirm the Aurora Borealis Aquapark and the 11-deck Venom Drop dry slide but do not confirm 'six' slides as a discrete number. Left unspecified.
discrepancies_flagged.godmother_tradition_break: MSC World Europa was notably the first MSC ship in years NOT christened by Sophia Loren. Her godmother was Sheikha Hind bint Hamad Al Thani, Vice-Chairperson and CEO of the Qatar Foundation, reflecting MSC's partnership with Qatar during the 2022 FIFA World Cup.
